pages.meta_description: Thrilled to be MP for East Grinstead, Uckfield a
headings: h2: Main navigation
h3: Mims Davies for East Grinstead & Uckfield
h2: Footer
pages.text_excerpt: About Mims Davies MP | Mims Davies Skip to main content Main navigation Home About Mims News Latest Newsletter National Campaigns & Statements Contact Sign up to my Newsletter! Surveys About Mims Davies MP Tweet Thrilled to be MP for East Grinstead, Uckfield and our villages with 19,319 residents putting their trust and support in me. I am a mum of two, and I have helped to care for my elderly parents through their ill health. I studied Politics at University and was the first person in my family to go, suffer the debts and succeed as a result of the opportunity! I served as a Councillor for almost 6 years in Mid Sussex at Parish, Town and District level and a Member of Parliament for a further 4 years in Eastleigh and know what it takes to make a difference in the community. You can find a record of my work in the new section archives on this website. I was very proud to have won twice this seat for our great party. I had a fantastic time doing this job and loved every minute. As my personal circumstances changed during that time, I chose not to rest and so a new candidate could carry the mantel forward. Coming home to Mid Sussex is fantastic as it means I can continue to make a difference in front line politics and be there for my daughters more after my divorce. During my time as a Councillor and as a keen sportswoman I founded the Mid Sussex Marathon Weekend, with three races run across East Grinstead, Haywards Heath and Burgess Hill, was a member of Haywards Heath Harriers and set up the thriving Haywards Heath Sports Forum. I loved being a Town Councillor and started my time on local councils when I was co-opted onto Hurstpierpoint and Sayers Common Parish Council. I have worked in a variety of fields - in retail, hospitality but mainly media, events and communications. Most notably for the AA, BBC and the Police. I most recently worked in Road Safety alongside the Police and their Partners where I helped reduce the number of people killed or seriously injured on the roads. That was a job I truly enjoyed, making a difference in the community and this outcome really matters to me. I am in politics as I care about people, community and doing what is right, and know this understanding and commitment is really important in modern politics. I do pride myself on speaking my mind and standing up for all those who need assistance. I am continuing my record of hard work, community action and reaching out to those who feel disconnected from politics. I love reading, history, creative writing, 80's pop music, pub quizzes, gin and tonics, anything fizzy to drink ideally followed by a curry! I am a member of the National Trust and enjoy getting involved with matters across the community large or small. I like volunteering and have been a trustee of a local veterans' charity.
